items.title: Game playing and simulation
items.description: RL agents master Atari, Go, chess, and strategy games by self-play and reward from wins/losses—environments with clear rules and simulatable dynamics.

items.title: Robotics and autonomous control
items.description: Robots learn locomotion, grasping, and navigation policies from reward signals based on task success, energy use, or safety constraints.

items.title: Recommendation and personalization
items.description: Bandit and contextual bandit formulations treat clicks or engagement as rewards, learning which items to show while exploring new content.

items.title: Resource management and operations
items.description: Data-center cooling, traffic signal timing, and inventory control use RL to optimize long-term cost and service-level objectives.

items.title: LLM alignment (RLHF)
items.description: Language models are fine-tuned with RL-style objectives using reward models trained on human preferences to improve helpfulness and safety.
